text.title: EEAT Signal Pass Template Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every EEAT Signal Pass Template application MUST enforce, plus a skip-this-methodology fallback. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Post MUST link to an author bio with credentials and topical expertise. Anonymous or generic 'staff' bylines are rejected.
rule.rationale: Google E-E-A-T weights authorship signals heavily; anonymous posts under-rank against named-author equivalents.

rule.statement: Post MUST display a last-updated date ≤12 months old. Stale or missing dates are rejected.
rule.rationale: Recency is a ranking factor for evergreen topics; older posts under-perform fresh ones.

rule.statement: Post MUST cite ≥3 sources with outbound links. Source-free posts are rejected.
rule.rationale: Citations signal research depth; AI engines preferentially cite citation-rich content.

rule.statement: Post MUST contain ≥1 original quote OR original data point. Aggregation-only posts are rejected.
rule.rationale: Original content is the strongest E-E-A-T signal; aggregation gets demoted.

rule.statement: Post MUST ship with article schema.org markup including author + datePublished + dateModified. Schema-free posts are rejected.
rule.rationale: Schema markup unlocks rich results and increases AI-engine extractability.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition / triggered skip, and route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Methodology hygiene: mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
